DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER TO FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION WITH REQUISITE PARTICULARITY. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT TOPWELL INVESTMENT INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHIFT ITS BURDEN AND SHOW THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO A JUDGMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. THE NOTICE OF MOTION/MOTION INDICATES THAT THIS MOTION IS BASED ON A COMPLETE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE AS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE UNTIMELY AND BARRED BY CCP 337.15 [SIC]. NO OTHER GROUNDS LISTED. SEE CRC 3.1110, 3.1112. IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES DEFENDANT RAISES ADDITIONAL GROUNDS: (1) CCP 337 AND 338; (2) LACK OF PROHIBITION AGAINST BANKING THE CPI ADJUSTMENTS; AND (3) LACK OF MISREPRESENTATION AND LACK OF ALLEGATION OF KNOWLEDGE OF FALSEHOOD AT THE TIME THE MISREPRESENTATION WAS MADE. THE LATTER TWO GROUNDS ARE NOT APPROPRIATE FOR A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS THEY FAIL TO DISPOSE OF THIS ACTION. AS TO THE STATUE OF LIMITATIONS, THE MOVING PARTY FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR A DISTINCT BREACH OF CONTRACT ACCRUES ON THE DATE OF A PRIOR SEPARATE AND DISTRICT BREACH OF THAT CONTRACT I.E. THAT FEBRUARY 1, 1995 ALLEGED VIOLATION OF THE LEASE (FAC, PARAGRAPH 15(A)) TRIGGERED THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS FOR THE SEPARATE, DISTINCT, AND DIFFERENT BREACH ALLEGED TO HAVE OCCURRED ON SEPTEMBER 17, 2014 (FAC, PARAGRAPH 23). ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: NEWTON J. LAM, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-31-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-13-17 At 08:00 A.M. In Dept 502

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TOPWELL INVESTMENT INC's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Production Of Documents And For Sanctions is GRANTED as follows: By October 17, 2016 (1) Plaintiff shall produce the documents or (2) Plaintiff shall produce verified response to the Request to Production stating that Plaintiff did not document any of her business losses occasioned by Defendant's alleged breaches. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $2,335. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Peter Balogh, Esq. (650-355-8834) for Plaintiff/ opposing party Susan Molthen.
Luanne Rutherford, Esq. (925-939-5330) for Defendant/Moving party TOPWELL INVESTMENT INC.

Both sides ARGUED. Defendant argues Statute of limitation. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TOPWELL INVESTMENT INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that it is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. The Notice of Motion/Motion indicates that this motion is based on a complete affirmative defense as Plaintiff's claims are untimely and barred by CCP 337.15 [sic]. No other grounds listed. See CRC 3.1110, 3.1112.
In the Points and Authorities Defendant raises additional grounds: (1) CCP 337 and 338; (2) lack of prohibition against banking the CPI Adjustments; and (3) lack of misrepresentation and lack of allegation of knowledge of falsehood at the time the misrepresentation was made. The latter two grounds are not appropriate for a Motion for Summary Judgment as they fail to dispose of this action.
As to the statue of limitations, the moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a cause of action for a distinct breach of contract accrues on the date of a prior separate and district breach of that contract i.e. that February 1, 1995 alleged violation of the lease (FAC, paragraph 15(A)) triggered the statute of limitations for the separate, distinct, and different breach alleged to have occurred on September 17, 2014 (FAC, paragraph 23). Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Newton J. Lam, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-13-2016 Set For Trial On Oct-31-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Off Calendar Mar-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Luanne Rutherford, (925)939-5330 for Defendant/Moving party Topwell Investment Inc.
Peter Balogh, (650)355-8834 for Plaintiff/Opposing party Susan Molthen Ind. and DBA Al-Masr.

Argued by counsel for the Denfendant. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT TOPWELL INVESTMENT INC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts to support causes of action for fraud and for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: A. James Robertson II Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Luanne Rutherford, (925)939-5330 for Defendant/Moving party Topwell Investment Inc.
Peter Balogh, (650)355-8834 for Plaintiff/Opposing party Susan Molthen Ind. and DBA Al-Masr.

Argued by counsel for Defendant. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT TOPWELL INVESTMENT INC's Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to paragraph 44, line 10-11. Motion is otherwise denied. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts to support punitive damages and disgorgement of excessive rents. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: A. James Robertson II Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Demurrer to cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing is sustained with leave to amend each element of this cause of action. Demurrer to fraud cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege each element of this cause of action with requisite particularity. Demurer to cause of action for interference with contractual relations, interference with prospective business advantage and conversion is sustained without leave to amend.
